Rust · Python · WebAssembly · C ABI

calib-targets-rs

Calibration-target detection in Rust. One grid-first algorithmic core finds a chessboard lattice, then decodes the anchors, dots, or circles in each rectified cell — so chessboards, ChArUco, PuzzleBoard, and marker boards all share a single detection pipeline and a common corner vocabulary. Partial views still decode to absolute IDs.

Guide

The mdBook: concepts, the topological grid pipeline, per-target detection, and a getting-started tutorial.

API reference

Generated cargo doc for every workspace crate — the facade, the grid core, and each detector.

Demo

Run the detectors in your browser via WebAssembly. Load a sample image or generate a synthetic target, then tune parameters live.

Performance

Per-image, per-stage timing on public sample boards — where the milliseconds go, measured rather than claimed.

Source on GitHub · vitavision.dev
